ucs.title: [LLM] ToddyCat Exchange/IIS w3wp.exe spawning Samurai/Ninja loader chain
ucs.description: Hunts ToddyCat's signature initial-access pattern: ProxyLogon-style exploitation of Microsoft Exchange (T1190) dropping a China Chopper-style ASPX webshell, with the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) then spawning cmd.exe/powershell.exe and side-loading a malicious DLL (the 'Samurai' or 'Ninja' backdoor staged via Microsoft.Exchange.* binaries). Kaspersky attributes this exact chain to ToddyCat (G1022) since 2020 against EU/APAC government and telecom targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_id) as pid values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*Samurai*","*Ninja*","*ServerHost*","*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*","*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval toddycat_score=case(match(process,"(?i)\\\\inetpub\\\\wwwroot\\\\aspnet_client|FrontEnd\\\\HttpProxy\\\\owa\\\\auth|Samurai|Ninja"),5, match(process,"(?i)whoami|net user|net group /domain|tasklist|systeminfo|nltest"),3, match(process_name,"(?i)rundll32|regsvr32") AND match(parent_process,"(?i)w3wp"),4, true(),1) | where toddycat_score>=3 | sort - toddycat_score firstTime
ucs.kql:
let exchange_paths = pack_array(@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\owa\auth\", @"\inetpub\temp\");
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","schtasks.exe")
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Samurai","Ninja","ServerHost","DismCore.dll","WmiPrvSE")
   or FolderPath has_any (exchange_paths)
| extend Recon = iif(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("whoami /all","net group \"Domain","net user /domain","nltest /domain_trusts","tasklist /v","systeminfo"), 1, 0)
| extend SideLoad = iif(FileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") and InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe", 1, 0)
| extend WebshellPath = iif(FolderPath has_any (exchange_paths) or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (exchange_paths), 1, 0)
| extend ToddyCatScore = Recon + (SideLoad*2) + (WebshellPath*2)
| where ToddyCatScore >= 2
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ToddyCatScore
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FolderPath has_any (exchange_paths) and FileName endswith ".aspx" | summarize WebshellWritten=make_set(FileName), WebshellPathSeen=make_set(FolderPath) by DeviceName) on DeviceName
| order by ToddyCatScore desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: ToddyCat's defining initial-access TTP — documented by Kaspersky in June 2022 — is dropping the China Chopper webshell into the Exchange aspnet_client/FrontEnd directories, then using w3wp.exe to launch loaders that side-load Samurai/Ninja DLLs. Stacking the IIS-process parent + recon-binary child + Exchange path + side-loading binary is far more specific to G1022 than any single technique alone;

ucs.title: [LLM] ToddyCat data staging in $RECYCLE.BIN with WinRAR password-archive then Dropbox/cloud exfil
ucs.description: Hunts ToddyCat's signature collection-and-exfil chain: archiving stolen data into password-protected RAR/ZIP files (T1560.001), staging the archives inside the local Recycle Bin to evade defenders (T1074.002), then exfiltrating over HTTPS to public cloud storage like Dropbox/Yandex/OneDrive (T1567.002). Kaspersky's October 2023 'Check Logs' report shows ToddyCat (G1022) reuses this exact tradecraft against government victims in EU/APAC.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","WinRAR.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe") AND Processes.process IN ("*-hp*","*-p*","* a *","*-r *","*-m5*","*-v*")) OR (Processes.process IN ("*$RECYCLE.BIN*","*\\$Recycle.Bin\\*")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval staging=if(match(process,"(?i)\\$RECYCLE\\.BIN|\\$Recycle\\.Bin"),1,0), pwarchive=if(match(process,"(?i)\\s-(hp|p)[^ ]+") AND match(process_name,"(?i)rar|7z"),1,0) | where staging=1 OR pwarchive=1 | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.dest IN ("*dropboxapi.com","*dropbox.com","content.dropboxapi.com","*yandex.net","*disk.yandex.*","*onedrive.live.com","*1drv.ms","*mega.nz","*pcloud.com") Web.http_method IN ("POST","PUT") by Web.src Web.dest Web.url Web.bytes_out | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | where bytes_out>1048576 | rename src as host] | stats values(*) as * by host | where (staging=1 OR pwarchive=1) AND isnotnull(dest) | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let archive_procs = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe"]);
let cloud_exfil = dynamic(["dropboxapi.com","dropbox.com","disk.yandex.net","yandex.net","onedrive.live.com","1drv.ms","mega.nz","pcloud.com"]);
let ArchiveStaging =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ (archive_procs)
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(hp|p)\S+"  // password flag
         or ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"$RECYCLE.BIN", @"$Recycle.Bin")
    | extend PasswordArchive = iif(ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(hp|p)\S+", 1, 0)
    | extend RecycleBinStage = iif(ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"$RECYCLE.BIN", @"$Recycle.Bin"), 1, 0)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, PasswordArchive, RecycleBinStage;
let RecycleWrites =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"$RECYCLE.BIN", @"$Recycle.Bin")
    | where FileName endswith ".rar" or FileName endswith ".7z" or FileName endswith ".zip" or FileName endswith ".cab" or FileName endswith ".dat"
    | summarize StagedFiles=make_set(FileName), StagedPaths=make_set(FolderPath) by DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 1h);
let CloudExfil =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (cloud_exfil) or RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (cloud_exfil)
    | summarize ExfilBytes=sum(toint(coalesce(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).bytes_sent), "0"))), ExfilHosts=make_set(RemoteUrl), ExfilCount=count() by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, bin(Timestamp, 1h);
ArchiveStaging
| join kind=leftouter (RecycleWrites) on DeviceName
| join kind=leftouter (CloudExfil) on DeviceName
| where (PasswordArchive == 1 and RecycleBinStage == 1) or (PasswordArchive == 1 and isnotempty(ExfilHosts)) or (isnotempty(StagedFiles) and isnotempty(ExfilHosts))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, PasswordArchive, RecycleBinStage, StagedFiles, ExfilHosts, ExfilBytes
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: The triple combination of (1) password-protected RAR archive creation, (2) the archive being written into the per-SID $RECYCLE.BIN folder, and (3) HTTPS POST/PUT to Dropbox/Yandex within the same host/session is uniquely characteristic of ToddyCat's documented post-exploitation playbook. Each component alone is noisy, but the three together are nearly pathognomonic for G1022 and rarely produced by
